track_description: Background refurbishment, disclosure, and standards pressure that gradually lowers commercial building-service energy intensity without introducing a separate package choice.
evidence_summary: Australian commercial-building guidance and AEMO business-demand forecasts support a modest background standards and disclosure drift on incumbent and partially electrified commercial service bundles.
derivation_method: Use one role-wide exogenous drift across incumbent and electrified-efficiency commercial methods after rebaselining those rows so the autonomous effect is explicit rather than hidden in the method table.
double_counting_guardrail: Do not apply to the deep-electric commercial method. The applicable role rows are rebaselined upward so the autonomous track preserves today's modeled pathway behavior instead of double counting it.
review_notes: Kept modest so the explicit standards drift preserves the prior role trajectory without crowding out the larger deep-electric pathway shift.
